programdescription: This one-year program prepares high school students in small, rural communities in the Columbia River Gorge region of Washington state for IT and Web Development careers. Upon completion of the program, the participating students will be prepared to sit for the CompTIA a+ certification.rtification) and/or Dual Credit in an articulated Career Launch program.

programdescription: CodeDay Labs is the 100% online tech internship for everyone. Mentors guide a team of 3 students as they contribute to a real-world open-source project in 3-7 weeks. Students completing the project will have a verifiable contribution to a real project with 10K-10M+ users. They will also receive career mentorship, resume help, and practice interviewing.

programdescription: Students enrolled in a Career and Technical Education (CTE) tech pathway course gain valuable hands-on experience that prepares them for careers in the rapidly evolving technology sector. These courses offer a blend of academic and practical learning, allowing students to develop technical skills in areas like coding, networking, cybersecurity, and software development. By working on real-world projects, they not only build a solid foundation in technology but also enhance their problem-solving and critical thinking abilities. CTE tech pathway courses include opportunities for PAID internships, certifications, and exposure to industry professionals, giving students a competitive edge as they transition to higher education or enter the workforce. These programs also play a crucial role in addressing the growing demand for skilled tech professionals in various industries.  Certification can include but not limited to: Microsoft Office Specialist (MOS), CompTia,

Our tuition-free cybersecurity training based in Seattle offers the opportunity to earn the Cybersecurity Analyst Certification (CySA+), equipping you with fundamental concepts in security specialties and hands-on training to enter this ever-growing field as a cybersecurity analyst. As a graduate of our cybersecurity training, you will earn the Cybersecurity Analyst Certification (CySA+), equipped with the knowledge to fulfill a wide range of entry-level technology jobs such as Cyber Security Analyst, Junior Desktop Technician, Tech Support Engineer, and more.

programdescription: This 15-week skills development training prepares individuals for positions in the cloud through real-world, scenario-based learning, labs, and coursework. Upon completion of the program, AWS re/Start graduates are suitable candidates for entry-level cloud computing jobs in areas such as cloud operations, site reliability, tier 1 infrastructure support, or DevOps.

target_job_roles: Network Administrators, Network Admins, Web Administrators, Cyber Security Administrators, Cyber Defenders, Cyber Forensics Investigators
hoursperweek: varies by students and their Cyber Club
programdescription: Our primary event is the Pacific Rim Collegiate Cyber Defense Competition (PRCCDC). It is the regional qualifier for the National CCDC. To prepare for the event students are given access to various training and some virtual competitions. Students learn how to defend a corporate network, work as a team and present to a Board of Directors. The students are able to put this on their CV.

target_job_roles: Project Manager, Software Analyst, Mobile Application Developer, Software Developer, Software Engineer/Site Reliability Engineer, Software Quality Assurance Engineer and Tester/SDET, Web Application Developer
targetpopulation: Part-time or full-time students, that leads to a bachelor degree
programdescription: In partnership with North Seattle College and powered by Riipen, Tech Talent Boost Washington offers students and recent alumni transformative internships in the tech sector. Through employer-led projects, participants gain hands-on experience and direct pathways to job placements so employers can hire the best-fit participants to join their team. Tailored career services and coaching empower learners to succeed in their tech careers, while facilitating Washington-based employers' access to exceptional talent by expanding their hiring practices. This partnership ensures participants are equipped with the skills and support they need to thrive in their tech careers.

target_job_roles: Junior network administrator~Data center support technician~Network engineer~System administrator
description: Focus on networking fundamentals, operations, troubleshooting, security, cloud computing, hardware. Training aligns with CompTIA Network+ and AWS Cloud Practitioner Foundational certifications.
costsummary: Most program costs are covered for students via VA benefits. Students without VA benefits or who demonstrate financial need may be eligible for financial assistance. Tuition: $22,450 Fees: $880* (Server and Cloud Application and Cloud Application Development pathways) Fees: $1,500* (Cybersecurity Administration pathway) Optional on-campus housing (Lacey, WA campus only): No cost for active duty using GI Bill, or any active duty traveling from overseas, or $2,895.00/four months for veterans and military spouses.

programdescription: Students focus on IT support, network administration, and software development, qualifying them for roles in tech support and network systems. Certifications include CompTIA A+, Network+, and Microsoft Certified Solutions Associate (MCSA).

Provides training for computer support technicians, security specialists, network administrators, and engineers. Completing the computer technology series prepares students for certifications like CompTIA A+, Network+, Linux, and MCSA.

Designed for the high-tech job market, combining electrical and automation principles with advanced math and science. The program aims to prepare graduates to effectively implement and complete complex repairs and upgrades.

Trains maintenance electricians and electronics technicians for various industrial settings, including data centers, food storage, manufacturing, and hydroelectric facilities. It also offers advanced training for data center and plant electricians and employees looking to enhance their skills in electronic circuits, PLCs, and control systems.

Prepares graduates for entry-level roles like service technicians and environmental systems designers in various settings, including data centers. Combining theory with hands-on lab work, the curriculum covers refrigeration, HVAC systems, and energy efficiency. Students can work full-time while studying, culminating in an internship and a capstone project tailored to their career goals.

The two-year Systems Administration option leads to Associate in Applied Science (AAS or AAS-T) degree that prepares students for immediate employment or continued education in the field of Information Technology. This degree also offers short term (one year or less) which are subsets of the two-year degree. Students may enroll in individual classes for retraining, updating skills, or preparing for IT industry certifications. Certificates are short-term training options that prepare students with the skills necessary to obtain entry level employment and complete computer industry certifications and exams. Certificate options available: Computer Support Specialist, Network Support Specialist, Cisco Networking. Students will also obtain their CompTIA A+ certification within the degree program.

programdescription: Students taking a Data Center Prep Academy course are immersed in the foundational aspects of data center operations, gaining crucial insights into the infrastructure that powers today’s digital world. These courses typically cover topics such as server management, networking, cooling systems, power distribution, and data security, equipping students with the technical skills needed to maintain and troubleshoot the complex environments of modern data centers. By participating in hands-on labs and simulations, students learn to manage physical hardware and virtual systems, preparing them for roles in IT support, network administration, or data center operations. The course often includes industry certifications, giving students a competitive advantage when seeking employment in a sector where reliability, efficiency, and security are paramount. As demand for data centers grows, these students are well-positioned to step into critical roles in the technology infrastructure space.

targetpopulation: North Central Washington residents of all ages.
servicearea: North Central Washington - Wenatchee School District
programdescription: SkillSource offers on-the-job training for new hires, upskilling training solutions for existing employees, and facilitates youth apprenticeships in computer support technician positions in coordination with the Wenatchee School District.

The study and practice of integrating traditional tools and equipment with modern technology into a single system. This may include working with robotics, communications (wired and wireless), electronics, motors, pumps, electricity, pneumatic equipment, hydraulics, and CNC equipment. Manufacturing and Process Technology provides students the ability to specialize their training to work in a variety of industry settings in our area including manufacturing facilities, data centers, food or chemical processing plants, and other businesses with need for regular facility or equipment maintenance. Areas of emphasis/certification include Maintenance, Mission Critical Operations, and Robotics/Automation.
